headline: Bajau Laut Statelessness Crisis Affects 45,000 People
context: An estimated 45,000 Bajau Laut sea nomads in eastern Sabah remain stateless lacking access to education healthcare and legal employment.

cards.big: 45,000 Bajau Laut in Sabah remain stateless without documentation
cards.sub: Sea nomad communities denied schools healthcare and legal work opportunities

cards.big: Only 12% of Bajau Laut children in Semporna district attend any school
cards.sub: UNHCR documented 8,500 stateless children in Sabah east coast 2025 survey

cards.big: NRD rejected 78% of Bajau Laut birth registration applications since 2020
cards.sub: Lack of parental documentation creates intergenerational statelessness cycle

cards.big: Sabah state spent RM45 million on healthcare for undocumented populations
cards.sub: Federal reimbursement covered only RM12 million leaving state to absorb rest

cards.big: Statelessness reveals whether citizenship is a birthright or a bureaucratic privilege
45,000 stateless Bajau Laut exist in a legal void that no single ministry can resolve alone.
